mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 130k signals robust labor market demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC > 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signals inflationary demand pressures, prompting central bank tightening that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation with combined GDPC1/CPIAUCSL growth in 60%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) above 500k signals improved consumer confidence and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 519.0.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with rising Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 0.25%) signals monetary tightening, which reduces equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show positive correlation with combined CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS increases in 60%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong household demand, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS changes over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE < 9.8%) signals tighter labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs that reduce equity earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in < 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 9.8.
